meta.title: South America + China Trip Plan
meta.publicSafetyNote: No booking references, passport details, private addresses, or contact details should be stored in this public repo.
assumptions: Steve returns to the United States on 2026-07-05 from Hong Kong.
China itinerary details after city/date level are pending Steve's afternoon update.
Packing advice is based on late-June and early-July seasonal conditions and should be refined with near-term forecasts before departure.

route.notes: Land at PVG in the afternoon. Keep evening light because this follows GRU-ADD-PVG long-haul travel.
Meetings currently proposed for morning and early afternoon China time.
Likely hot, humid, and rainy. Keep one fast-dry outfit ready.
Warmer and potentially drier than the Yangtze Delta, but still summer heat.
Leave one nicer outfit available for meetings or dinners.
Hot, very humid, and likely thunderstorm risk. Prioritize breathable layers and waterproof carry.
Similar to Shenzhen/Hong Kong: hot, humid, showers possible.
Hot and humid. Keep passport, onward ticket, and cross-border essentials easy to reach.
Buffer day before U.S. return. Do laundry or repack here if needed.
Return-to-U.S. day. Keep U.S. entry documents and one clean flight outfit in carry-on.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Hot and humid, with plum-rain-season shower risk.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Use breathable shirts, fast-dry pants, light rain shell or compact umbrella, and shoes that can handle wet pavement.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Summer heat, usually less humid than Shanghai/Hangzhou.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Short sleeves work during the day; keep one smarter layer for indoor AC and meetings.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Very hot and humid, with thunderstorms or typhoon-season rain possible.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Prioritize quick-dry clothes, sandals or waterproof-friendly shoes, compact umbrella, and a dry bag for electronics.

weatherAssumptions.expected: Southern Hemisphere winter: Buenos Aires and Sao Paulo can be cool; Rio is milder.
weatherAssumptions.packingImpact: Do not pack only summer clothes. Bring a light jacket or overshirt that also works on long flights.

packingPlan.strategy: Pack for two climates: cool South America winter plus hot, humid China summer. Re-wear outer layers, wash quick-dry basics, and keep one meeting-ready outfit.

items: 1 long-sleeve overshirt or thin merino/base layer
1 lightweight button-down or smart casual shirt
baseline.notes: Enough for humid China without overpacking; quick-dry fabric is better than thick cotton.

items: 1 shorts or very light casual pants
baseline.notes: Avoid jeans as the main pant because China south will be hot and wet.

items: 1 light jacket, overshirt, or packable windbreaker
1 compact rain shell or small umbrella
baseline.notes: Jacket covers South America evenings, airports, and heavy indoor AC in China.
Plan to do one laundry round in Shanghai/Hong Kong or wash quick-dry items in the sink.
Walking shoe should handle rain. Secondary shoe helps if the first pair gets wet.

items: One clean T-shirt and underwear in carry-on
baseline.notes: Keep this with the travel backpack, not checked luggage.

items: Light waterproof pouch or dry bag
baseline.notes: Dry bag matters for South China/HK rain and cross-city transfers.

optional: Swim shorts if beach/pool time is likely in Rio or Hong Kong
Small medicine kit: stomach meds, painkiller, allergy meds, bandages
Foldable tote for carrying things back from China
One extra empty packing cube for gifts/documents
openQuestions: Any formal meetings, dinners, or events in Shanghai/Beijing/Shenzhen/HK?
Checked luggage size and airline baggage limits for each segment?
Whether Steve needs to bring gifts, product samples, documents, or electronics back to China.
